reasoning: KMB saw an earnings-related price spike in early August (peaking around Aug 7) but has exhibited strong downward momentum over the past week, unwinding the pop back toward its pre-earnings baseline amid analysts slashing 2025 revenue estimates by 12-16% due to divestitures. This creates a strong near-term headwind, bringing the Aug 24 probability under 0.50. Furthermore, an upcoming $1.26 quarterly dividend with an ex-dividend date of Sept 5, 2025 will mechanically drop the unadjusted stock price by roughly 1%, creating a structural headwind for the Sep 16 and subsequent resolution dates. Longer-term, analysts maintain 12-month price targets around $141-$142, and general market drift tends to be positive, which slowly pulls the probabilities on the 3-month and 6-month horizons closer to 0.50 due to increased uncertainty and time for the stock to rebound.
